name: Wildfowl Stock
recipeIngredient: 5 pounds Game bird carcass (including wings, legs, neck, and other parts)
2 or 3 stalks Wild celery, chopped into chunks
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the cold water in a soup pot.
2. Add the game bird pieces to the cold water and soak for 1 hour.
3. Add the remaining ingredients (celery, onions, carrots, and watercress).
4. Do not stir; let it soak for another 1 hour.
5. Place the pot, uncovered, at the back of a cook stove, away from the fire.
6. On a conventional stove, place the pot on an asbestos pad over very low heat.
7. Allow the pot and its contents to warm slowly.
8. Skim off scum as it rises.
9. When the pot is uniformly warm, move it closer to the fire (or increase the heat) and gently bring the liquid to a simmer.
10. Do not boil; do not rush. Skim off all scum immediately after 30 minutes of simmering.
11. After 30 minutes and after skimming, wipe the pot's surface at the level of the liquid using clean cheesecloth.
12. Simmer gently, uncovered, for at least 3 hours.
13. Carefully ladle the stock through moistened cheesecloth into a large jar with a screw lid. Do not pour; leave the strained stock uncovered until cool.
14. Cover cooled stock tightly and store in the refrigerator.
15. Skim grease from the stock before reheating for use or freezing.
This recipe is from the cookbook on wild foods, as written because of its unique style and interesting ingredients. When using wild ingredients, be cautious, as some may have toxic look-alikes. Personally, I recommend sourcing wild ingredients at the supermarket to avoid potential hazards.
